column1: quinoa — grain with specific nutritional claims
column4: {"category":"Grain (pseudocereal) / Pantry staple","typical_unit":"1 lb (454 g) bag or 16–48 oz (454 g–1.36 kg)","storage_tip":"Unopened: store in a cool, dry pantry in a sealed package. column10: {"category":"whole grain (seed)","typical_unit":"1 lb bag or 12 oz box","storage_tip":"Unopened quinoa can be stored in a cool, dry pantry. Once opened, keep it tightly sealed in an airtight container in the pantry away from heat and moisture; for longer storage, refrigerate or freeze to help prevent rancidity and insect exposure.","nutrition_note":"Its standout trait is that it’s a complete plant protein, meaning it contains all nine essential amino acids. column1: tofu — refrigerated item with specific storage needs
column10: {"category":"Plant-based protein / soy food","typical_unit":"14 to 16 oz block (about 400 to 450 g)","storage_tip":"Unopened tofu can be stored in the refrigerator until the use-by date. Once opened, keep it submerged in fresh water in an airtight container in the fridge and change the water daily; use within 3 to 5 days. For longer storage, freeze unopened or pressed tofu, then thaw in the fridge before using.","nutrition_note":"Its most distinctive trait is that it’s a high-protein, soy-based food with very little saturated fat; it also provides iron and calcium (especially if calcium-set), though exact amounts vary by type."}
